2605.20314.title: Less Data, Faster Training: repeating smaller datasets speeds up learning via sampling biases
2605.20314.paper_summary: This work investigates the ``small-vs-large gap'', where repeating on fewer samples can lead to compute saving during training compared to using a larger dataset. This is observed across algorithmic tasks, architectures and optimizers and cannot be explained using prior theory. We argue that the speedup comes from appropriate layer-wise growth enabled by sampling biases, which is more pronounced when the dataset size is smaller. We provide both theoretical analysis and empirical evidence from various interventions. Our results suggest that using a smaller dataset with more repetitions is not just a fallback strategy under data scarcity, but can be proactively leveraged as a favorable inductive biases for optimization, particularly in reasoning tasks.
2605.20314.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作探討了「小樣本與大樣本之間的差距」，即在訓練過程中，重複較少樣本可以相較於使用較大數據集來節省計算資源。這一現象在各種算法任務、架構和優化器中均有觀察到，且無法用先前的理論來解釋。我們主張，這種加速來自於由取樣偏差所促成的適當層次增長，當數據集大小較小時，這一現象更加明顯。我們提供了理論分析和來自各種干預的實證證據。我們的結果表明，使用較小的數據集並進行更多重複不僅僅是在數據稀缺下的應急策略，而是可以主動利用作為優化的有利歸納偏見，特別是在推理任務中。
2605.20314.author: Jingwen Liu et.al.
2605.20314.authors: Jingwen Liu, Ezra Edelman, Surbhi Goel, Bingbin Liu
